text:
There was once a little girl named Melony who lived in a quiet town. She loved dressing up tiny dolls she found in antique shops. Every afternoon, Melony would arrange them on her shelf.

One day, while rummaging through her treasure trove of dolls, Melony stumbled upon a peculiar-looking teddy bear. It wasn't just any teddy bear—it had shiny buttons atop its head instead of fur. Curious, she pressed a button, and the bear let out a soft, melodious giggle.

Enchanted, Melony named it Buddy. Soon, Buddy became her confidant. Every evening, after her dolls had gone to sleep, Buddy would chat about everything. They talked about adventures, remembered lost childhood toys, and pondered dreams.

Then one evening, as Melony sat playing dolls, Buddy clicked his button and sang a whimsical song. Melony laughed warmly and sang back. It was as if Buddy could hear her dreams. Soon, they
